summary: Five-piece batch (signed asset URLs, WS tickets, DELETE-cancel pipeline, Whisper cost, PPTX hero + build-campaign) verified end-to-end. Backend: 14/14 pytest cases pass (1 skipped — no existing campaign creative had a media_url to inspect). Frontend: Assets page shows Whisper cost badge ($0.001 violet badge with testid asset-whisper-cost-{id}) and the Build Campaign button (testid asset-build-campaign, violet w/ Rocket icon) renders next to Re-analyze and Delete on the PPTX detail view as designed.

minor.issue: Pre-fix calendar posts (rows created before _abs_media_url() switched to signed URLs) still have unsigned relative media_url paths (no ?token=&exp=). Social dispatchers fetching those URLs without a session cookie would 403. New pushes mint signed URLs (verified by main agent). Consider a one-time backfill of existing cortex push rows OR document that pre-2026-05-30 pushes must be re-pushed before remote fetch.

integration_issues.issue: useHitlInbox.js attempts ws://...?token=<raw session_token> first (visible in browser console: 'WebSocket connection to wss://.../api/ws/hitl-inbox?token=test_session_1779636592168 failed'). Falls back to ws-ticket on next mount per main agent's design, but the initial failed-handshake warning shows up in console on every page load even when auth is fine. Recommend always minting a ws-ticket regardless of whether readCookie returned a value (HttpOnly cookies are unreadable in real browsers anyway, so the cookie path is mostly a dev-mode artifact).

action_items: (Optional) Backfill pre-fix cortex push rows in /posts to have signed media_url, or surface a re-push CTA for stale posts.
(Optional) Always use /api/auth/ws-ticket from useHitlInbox.js to silence the initial failed-handshake console warning in production browsers where HttpOnly truly blocks readCookie.
critical_code_review_comments: make_signed_asset_url() correctly clamps ttl 60s ≤ ttl ≤ 7 days and uses HMAC-SHA256(key|exp) — verified tampered-token / different-key / expired-token all return 403.

_signing_secret() derives from EMERGENT_LLM_KEY by default — fine for this env, but if EMERGENT_LLM_KEY ever rotates, all existing signed URLs in pushed posts will instantly 403. Consider a dedicated ASSET_SIGNING_SECRET env that's rotated on its own cadence.
/auth/ws-ticket persists single-use=true tickets in user_sessions and _auth_websocket() deletes them on first auth — replay correctly fails (verified via websockets lib).

cancel_pipeline() pops the asyncio.Task from _RUNNING_PIPELINES and calls .cancel(); DELETE handler returns {pipeline_cancelled: bool}. Note: as the review_request flagged, sub-second DELETE response is still gated by LLM event-loop blocking inside compose_artifacts — this is a known SEPARATE issue and out-of-scope for this batch.

build_campaign_from_asset reuses an existing brief (upsert on asset_id) and always invokes build_campaign() — idempotent on the brief side only, not on the campaign side (each call creates a fresh campaign row). UI should debounce the Build Campaign button to avoid double-clicks creating duplicate campaigns.

Asset detail Whisper cost badge correctly hidden when transcription_cost_usd is 0 (silent/failed transcription path); only renders for >0. Badge formats as '$0.001' via 3-decimal toFixed — matches main agent's smoke note.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (Whisper badge + Build Campaign button verified visually)

seed_data_creation: None — reused existing fixtures PPTX 52a3b704... + video 3b8e06e6... Build-campaign endpoint NOT exercised for happy path to save LLM/Gemini credits (main agent already smoke-verified). Only the 404-on-unknown-asset branch is asserted here.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 5 batch items are PASSING. Test suite at /app/backend/tests/test_cortex_iter27_signed_urls_ws_build.py is regression-safe and takes ~2s. WebSocket single-use test uses a fresh event loop per connect (asyncio.new_event_loop) to avoid 'event loop already running' under pytest 9. The TestSignedUrlsInCampaignCreatives test will START PASSING once at least one campaign with creatives is freshly pushed post-fix; currently skips because the fixture user only has pre-fix posts.
rca of the issue: No bug found in this iteration. Two follow-ups noted: (1) pre-existing pushed posts have stale unsigned media_url — only impacts users with existing un-republished cortex pushes; (2) HITL WS bootstrap logs a benign handshake-failure warning before falling back to the ws-ticket path — cosmetic only.
